dispersion_metric_note: HEADLINE dispersion = eig_trace (total variance) + eig_mpcd (mean pairwise cosine distance), rank-deficiency-ROBUST at K=16<<d (independent reviewers converged). eig_logdet/eig_pr retained for continuity but RIDGE-DOMINATED at K<<d -> NOT reliable, do NOT headline. EigenScore measures ANSWER-SAMPLE dispersion (INSIDE), NOT intrinsic representational occupancy (un-estimable at K<<d).

HEADLINE_TWINS_trap_vs_correct_conf_matc._note: THE rigorous geometric test: |margin|-quantile-binned AUROC (confidence held fixed by binning). ~0.5 => trap and correct are geometrically IDENTICAL twins (confident-error dispersion is REPRESENTATIONAL, not dynamical -- supports B-ADJ + PR(J)-invariance at the geometry level). Away from 0.5 => a genuine geometric difference. tuple = (auroc, n_used). Robust metrics first.

committed_vs_ignorance_RAW_CONFOUNDED_ex._warn: CONFOUNDED by |margin| BY CONSTRUCTION (committed==confident). independent review verdict: this CANNOT be cleanly de-confounded (residualizing on confidence regresses out the group). QUALITATIVE / exploratory ONLY -- never report as a clean separation. absm_medians exposes the confound.

gmax_structure_audit._q: is G_max INVARIANT (like PR) or STRUCTURED (varies w/ category/dispersion)? generic!=invariant

compare_to_e20d: e20d/R-1/R-4: raw G_max REFUTED as detector at 3B on wrong-vs-correct (gain_vs_norm~1, generic, AUROC~0.5). Here: does the {committed vs IGNORANCE} split and/or the /PR(J) ratio change that? + is G_max structured (CV>0, category/dispersion-correlated) rather than invariant?
VERDICT_proposed: TWINS (trap vs correct, confidence-matched, ROBUST dispersion): trace AUROC=0.407, mpcd AUROC=0.409. TWINS NULL: confident-wrong is geometrically INDISTINGUISHABLE from correct at matched confidence -> confident-error dispersion is REPRESENTATIONAL, not dynamical (supports B-ADJ + geometry-level PR-invariance). committed-vs-ignorance contrast is CONFOUNDED by confidence (un-de-confoundable per independent review) -> raw only, qualitative.
